Empires & Puzzles Wayne Review: 5-Star Legendary Holy Hero Guide

Is Wayne the trick or the treat of the Morlovia portal? We dissect this Very Fast bat to see if his heal-stealing antics can survive the modern stat wars or if he's just another spooky disappointment.
Wayne Review: The Bat Who Failed to Bleed?
Wayne: The Bat Who Failed to Bleed?

Meet Wayne, the "Great Magician of Morlovia," who burst onto the scene in the 2024 Halloween event looking like a bat that’s had a bit too much pumpkin spice latte. As a 5-star Holy Wizard from the Morlovia family, he arrived with a promise of speed and disruption. Released during a time when players were clamoring for game-changers, Wayne landed with more of a squeak than a screech. While his design is undeniably quirky—sporting a top hat and a manic grin—his kit has left many players wondering if the joke is on them.

Wayne’s artwork is a chaotic delight. He’s a bat-creature in a yellow suit, wielding a pumpkin wand and an expression that suggests he’s about to pull a rabbit out of a hat—or maybe just bite the rabbit. It’s a whimsical design that fits the Morlovia carnival theme perfectly. Unlike the brooding vampires or terrifying zombies of the family, Wayne looks like the eccentric uncle who ruins the family photo. Points for style, even if he looks a bit like he belongs in a cereal commercial rather than a battlefield.
Here is a look at Wayne's base and limit broken stats.
Let’s be brutally honest: Wayne’s stats have aged like milk left out in the Morlovia sun.
In the context of early 2026, where modern "Construct" and "Astral" heroes boast base HP pushing 2300 and attacks over 1300, Wayne is incredibly fragile. His LB2 stats barely catch up to the

stats of the latest heroes. With under 1800 HP even when fully limit broken (LB2), he is essentially a "glass cannon" without the cannon part. He is "glass confetti." You will need to protect him aggressively if you expect him to survive more than a stiff breeze.

Absorbs 30% of that bleed as health. Since the damage is low, the healing is low. It keeps him topped up against minion hits, perhaps, but won't save him from a sniper.

healing applied to enemies for 2 turns.
The concept is fantastic—punishing heavy healers like Hathor, Diaochan, or the Toon Vivicas. However, the duration is the killer.

the enemy healer. Since he is Very Fast, you can theoretically cycle him, but if the board dries up for even one turn, the effect expires, and the enemy heals back to full. It’s a high-skill, high-stress mechanic that often yields low rewards compared to just bringing a hero who kills the enemy outright.

60% theft means if an enemy healer tries to heal 1000 HP, they only get 400, and Wayne gets 600 (capped at his max HP, likely).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family and passives are the glue holding this kit together.

(+3%/+6%/+9%). This is excellent for Wayne. With just one other family member (like Matilda or Alucard), he gets +6% mana gen, making it even easier to hit that 6-tile break point with lower-level troops.

A 65% chance to steal the newest dispelable buff. This is actually very strong. Stealing a Taunt or a Dodge can swing a match. If no buffs are present, he deals a tiny bit of extra damage (150% to all). It adds a layer of RNG utility that can sometimes save you.

Heals for 100% of normal damage dealt for 6 turns. This is a survival tool. Since his tile damage is high (Yellow stack), this can keep him alive at the start of the battle, mitigating his low base HP issues slightly.
Released in 2024, Wayne was immediately overshadowed by his portal-mates like Alucard (Costume) and Chester. The community branded him "food" almost instantly due to the low bleed numbers.
The gap has only widened. His DoT damage hasn't scaled, while enemy HP pools have exploded. A 200 damage tick on a 3500 HP Toon hero is statistically irrelevant.

in standard wars against passive, healer-heavy teams. If you see a team with 3 healers, Wayne can disrupt them constantly. But for general play? He’s too weak.
Note: Wayne does not currently have a costume. If he ever gets one, pray for a stat boost.

in Rush because everyone else is Fast too.

His animation is fast, but his damage is slow. Not recommended.
Wayne needs protection and synergy to work. He is a support hero, not a killer.
Maximize the family mana bonus. Matilda extends the duration of ailments, which fixes Wayne's biggest flaw (the 2-turn duration).
Stack healing reduction/stealing and mana control to completely shut down a defensive team.
Any hero who can resist ailments makes Wayne useless. Toon passives (75% resist) are a hard counter.
Rigard, Toon Vivica, etc., wipe his effects instantly.

If Wayne is your first 5-star, he is... tricky. He won't carry you through map stages like a pure damage dealer. Level him if you have no other Yellow 5-stars, but don't prioritize him over solid 4-stars like Li Xiu or Waddles.

He is likely a "Soul Exchange" candidate for you. Unless you are playing around with a specific Matilda synergy team or need a very fast war flag for cleanup, his stats are just too low to justify the Alpha Aethers. Save your mats.
The community was not kind to Wayne upon release.

- A forum user succinctly capturing the mood.
"I’m one of those people who appreciates a hero who doesn’t do a million things at once. But... he just really doesn’t do anything at this point."
Many players compared him unfavorably to

Wayne is a classic example of a cool concept let down by conservative numbers. The idea of a Very Fast healing thief is terrifying, but the execution—low bleed and short duration—makes him more of a nuisance than a threat. Combined with stats that have been left in the dust by power creep, Wayne is unfortunately more of a trick than a treat. Keep him at 3/70 if you must, but he’s mostly waiting for a costume or a balance update.
